author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

`Storage::add_dep_edge` learns a cycle preflight for
`DepKind::Blocks` (the one edge kind `jjf ready` walks).
Before landing the commit, it runs a forward DFS from
`target` over existing `blocks` edges; if `source` is
reachable, the new edge would close a cycle and we reject
with the new `Error::DependencyCycle { from, target, cycle }`
variant. The `cycle` field carries the path
`[target, ..., source]` so the CLI envelope can echo which
existing edges are involved.

`cli::Error::DependencyCycle` mirrors the variant; the
storage error maps to CLI `kind: "dependency_cycle"`, exit
code 2 (preflight), with `details: {source, target, cycle}`.
The cycle walk runs inside the `mutate` closure so a CAS-loss
retry re-evaluates against the fresh post-race graph. Closed
issues are still nodes in the DFS — closing one node in a
cycle can't open a backdoor.

Scope: `blocks` only. `parent-child` cycles are nonsense too
(and harmful for `jjf dep tree`, though that walker already
has its own visited-set guard), but ready-set impact is the
load-bearing case. Extending cycle detection to `parent-child`
is a follow-up; the DFS helper `find_blocks_cycle` is the
right place to generalize.

Files touched: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`,
`crates/jjf/src/main.rs`, `docs/cli-json.md`,
`crates/jjf-storage/tests/integration.rs`,
`crates/jjf/tests/dep.rs`.

Tests: 6 new storage integration tests (direct 2-cycle, indirect
3-cycle, diamond non-cycle + idempotent re-add, rm-then-re-add,
non-blocks kinds skip the check, closed nodes still checked) +
1 new CLI integration test pinning the `dependency_cycle`
envelope shape. Full workspace nextest run is green.

Manual repro from the ticket now exits 2 with the expected
envelope:

```
$ jjf --json dep add $A $C
{"ok":false,"error":{"kind":"dependency_cycle","message":"adding blocks-edge f0914fc -> 07a43b6 would close a dependency cycle","details":{"source":"f0914fc","target":"07a43b6","cycle":["07a43b6","c803e56","f0914fc"]}}}
$ echo $?
2
```

# Recommendation

Land. Write-time prevention is the higher-value half of the
two fix candidates in the ticket. The read-time audit
candidate (`jjf ready` warns on unreachable issues caused by
cycles) is still worth doing but smaller-priority now: with
the write path closed, the only way a cycle lands is through
direct ref manipulation, a pre-fix corpus, or a future bug
in `jjf new -d` (the on-create form doesn't yet cycle-check).

# Confidence

High. The 6 storage tests pin every interesting shape (direct,
indirect, diamond, idempotent re-add, kind isolation, closed
nodes); the CLI test pins the envelope contract. Manual repro
matches the ticket exactly.

# Open follow-ups

1. Extend cycle detection to `DepKind::ParentChild` (would
   tighten `jjf dep tree` and prevent inheritance loops). The
   DFS helper is parameterizable; one extra dispatch case.
2. Extend cycle detection to the on-create path
   (`Storage::create_issue` honoring `IssueDraft::dependencies`).
   Less urgent: the new issue's id is brand-new so the smallest
   cycle reachable via `-d` requires two pre-existing edges
   pointing at it, which can't exist before creation.
3. The read-time audit candidate from the ticket (`jjf ready`
   warns when open issues are unreachable due to a cycle).
   Useful for cleaning up pre-fix corpora.
